entry.content:

Content enricher in SAP Integration suite can be used to enrich the content. In this we can use Original message / Lookup message to compare the field which can be used to enrich. Can you please advise if we can use multiple fields while using Original message / Lookup message.

For ex: Payload 1,

<A_CustomerMaterial>
<A_CustomerMaterialType>
<Customer>1000000</Customer>
<DistributionChannel>10</DistributionChannel>
<SalesOrganization>1000</SalesOrganization>
<Material>2</Material>

<Material>4</Material>

<DistributionChannel>10</DistributionChannel>
<SalesOrganization>1000</SalesOrganization>

<Customer>1000000</Customer>
<DistributionChannel>12</DistributionChannel>
<SalesOrganization>1100</SalesOrganization>

<DistributionChannel>10</DistributionChannel>
<SalesOrganization>1000</SalesOrganization>

Above is dummy example created for what I am looking to achieve

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Intercompany payment runs through an international ac...
entry.content:
Hi @Lynsey_Wardlaw.

Thank you for your question. The basic subscription for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition comes with pre-activated scope items for 

  • Basic Bank Account Management (BFA),
  • Bank Integration with File Interface (1EG). 
Both of these scope items are available for UK and the Netherlands.
Intercompany postings - while common for some of our customers - are not considered part of the basic subscription and therefore are not pre-activated by default. Some scope items may be activated with a simple activation request, like e.g. Intercompany Financial Posting (1GP), or Intercompany Reconciliation Process (40Y). Others may require additional licensing before submitting an activation request. For licensing additional scope items please reach out to your (customer's) SAP account executive. For scope item activation please follow SAP Note 33122430. 
 
SAP's solution and product managers monitor closely which scope items should be included in basic pre-activated solution packages, which ones shall become available only upon request, and which ones deliver higher levels of customer value and may deserve their own respective pricing. They do this while considering SAP's competitors' offerings as well, if any. Product managers might be eager to learn more about your specific scenarios and reach out to you if you don't mind. 
 
To suggest specific product improvements, functional enhancements or revise pricing considerations please submit your new suggestion through the SAP Customer Influence Program, here, or support respective suggestions by others via up-voting and commentary. You can also reach out to your SAP Customer Success Partner, your implementation partner, and further the scope of your influence by engaging with your regional SAP User Group. For the UK and Ireland, for example, that might be UKISUG. Check out SAP user groups here.              

 If this solves your request please mark accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Positive Pay Output File Format
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @Kim_anne.

Due to the behavior of different web browsers, the file may be saved with a different extension. For example, Google Chrome may save the file with an XML extension. After saving the file, you can rename the file to a TXT extension. (Source: SAP for Me | SAP Signavio Process Navigator | SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | Scope Item "Bank Integration with File Interface (1EG)", 1EG Test Script (UK/EN) , Chapter 4.1.) 

If you encounter following symptoms after payment media file download you may need to change the "Alternative Format Type" from 90 SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity to No Alternative Format Type through SSCUI 101972 Set Up Payment Methods per Country for Payment Transactions:

  1. XML file is blank/empty;
  2. XML file send to the bank is rejected with the error "Invalid File Format";
  3. The special character "#" appears at the starting of the XML file;

4. Encoding in payment media file is changed to UTF-16 from UTF-8. When the file is sent to bank, the bank doesn't accept it as the encoding is UTF-16. 

If this solves your request, please mark accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Missing decimals with MOVE-CORRESPONDING of CURR type
entry.content:
I have a FM where I importing it_tab1. This it_tab1 refers to ztable1.

From other side I use a structure is_str1 that refers to standard table ztable2 with similar structure to ztable1.

Both ztable1 and ztable2 has field zfield with data type CURR (11+2).

Then i do

 

 

 

LOOP AT it_tab1 ASSIGNING <it_tab1>

...

CLEAR is_str1.
MOVE-CORRESPONDING <it_tab1> TO is_str1.

 

 

 

And then I see in DEBUGGER that ZFIELD with same type missing decimal part.

Please suggest what should be a reason of this an how to fix it?

P.S. Fixed point arithmetic flag on GF level is on.

entry.title: Unknown Job added to Process Chain and unable to delete step
entry.content:
Recently we were updating the schedule on one of our Process Chains to utilize the calendar function. We tested in our Dev environment and moved it to QA. After moving to QA and testing the product, we found there was an additional step in the process chain. Anytime we try to open anything with in the process chain we get an error Variant &00000024024709 is inconsistent/incomplete in the database (see long text). I get about eight of those pop ups all with a slightly different number. I have attached a screenshot of error. We have tried deleting the step but am unable to. We have tried un-scheduling everything in the system. After checking the community, we found Note 2615955 and tried all of those steps. We tried moving it back down to Devn and back to QA again. None of those have helped. We even tried refreshing our QA system from Prod to wipe out the changes and try moving the change again. That actually made the issue worse now every process chain has the additional step in it.

Has anyone else had this issue? If so how were you able to resolve it? 

If it matters we are on SAP NETWEAVER 7.5

entry.title: Re: Query on PageSize Property in S/4 HANA Public edition...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @rahuljain257.

You may want to try out the API with its many segments in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (SAP Business Accelerator Hub). For example with segment A_BusinessPartner. First try with the $top set to 50 as is the default.

Press 'Run'. 

Take note of the Request URL in the response. I got "https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$top=50&$inlinecount=allpages".

In the response body note field 

"__count": "7057"

This is the number of all records for that segment. That number helps you build expectations if and when you decide to adjust the number of records in $top. You can use parameter $top like a paginator, and even leave it out, but only up to a point. For illustration, try again with the 50 removed. The API might not execute: "Error! Execution failed please try again".  

Now try with a different segment, say, the first one in the list on the left, A_AddressEmailAddress. Remove the '50' in $top, but keep $inlinecount with value "allpages", that ensures you get the __count in the results. 

If you need all the records to a particular segment, you may first want to get an understanding of the response behavior of the API for that segment. Just try with $top = 1 and $inlinecount = "allpages". Then go with approximation for the number of records you get returned - if any - when leaving $top blank: 

For example, for A_BusinessPartner and $top I entered ever lower 4 digit numbers starting with 7000, decrementing by 1000 until I got to a number that yielded results again, '1000'. Then I increased the number again, here to 1500 (OK) then 1900 (Error), 1600 (Error). The cut-off number for this segment must be between 1500 and 1600. I also noticed, getting 1500 records cost me about 5 seconds (against a performance-limited sandbox system, please test for your customer system, too).

For the values of all records of a segment you may then want to use "pagination" deliberately with stability/reliability and performance criteria in mind. You'd hop from page to page by setting respective parameter $skip, accordingly, e.g. at 1500, with $top 1500 (or lower, and respectively more pages, but better per-page performance).       

Enjoy 'playing' with the different segments. 

If this solves your request, please mark accordingly. Thank you, Rahul.

entry.title: Be yourself.. be honest.. and ask your questions
entry.content:
It's been a while ago. I was asked in an interview what I think the company does and why I think I'm a good fit.

Usually I responded with some of my experience which is a match to the offered job, but this time I had a second job already in hand and so there was no need to give this answer:

"I think you read my cv, so I'm pretty sure you know the answer to this question, if you want me to repeat it, I'm happy to do so, but please let me ask also a question. Why do you think this is the right place for me to grow?"

It was the best question I ever asked. I got a 30 minute presentation how the company want to make progress on projects and also to define kpi's. After that I had the chance to talk to some people and I also got a better offer..

Don't know if it will happen again, but if I ever will do a interview again I think I will ask this a second time 🙂

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Q2 2024 has been a whirlwind of activity! We've made substantial advancements to our product with updates such as robust AI capabilities, scalable sandboxes and the introduction of Consumption Credits, all aimed at increased flexibility for your commerce operations.

We took center stage at our annual SAP Sapphire event, proudly showcasing how SAP is leading the way in Business AI. We shined a spotlight on how esteemed customers like Nestlé are leveraging SAP's offerings to provide seamless experiences, coupled with the latest updates on our Intelligent CX portfolio and roadmap. You can discover the many CX highlights by watching the on-demand replays.

As we wind down the first half of the year, we'd like to give you an early heads-up about our 2024 Holiday Season Readiness Program in preparation for the year-end holidays. You must register by August 30, 2024 to participate in this free program.

SAP recently earned the TrustRadius Top Rated and G2 awards for commerce which are a testament to our unwavering commitment to our customers and the trust and value our customers have experienced with SAP Commerce Cloud. We also listen to customer feedback. As an example, the self-service pod restart feature highlighted in this blog was a top requested feature in our SAP Customer Influencer tool.

Let's now dive into the top features that have been rolled out in the past three months for SAP Commerce Cloud. To see our product in action, please view the Q2 2024 Release Highlights webcast to learn more about our latest innovations.

 

Industry-Tailored

Leverage AI to streamline sales of complex products

Let's talk about SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation (SAP IPR), our advanced, cloud-native Business AI tool specially designed for manufacturers and distributors dealing with complex products.

SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation is like the ultimate smart friend we all wish we had. It uses historical data, paired with machine learning, to suggest the most suitable standard or customizable products. But it doesn’t stop there. It can also offer recommendations on areas like default configuration options, price estimates, delivery timeline, total cost of ownership, and even factors like weight - all tailored around the customer’s initially stated needs.

Let’s take a distributor example. The distributor needs to update some of the vehicles in their fleet and desires fuel efficiency and the means to keep products refrigerated in the cab. Now with SAP IPR, instead of configuring technical options, the distributor can specify needs and preferences and receive recommendations of options such as engine types, engine power, cab sizes, cooling systems, and more.

We also provide an intuitive NoCode app builder. This easy-to-use tool empowers business users to create need-based recommendation models that blend human intelligence with machine intelligence. What’s in it for you? Here comes the fun part:

  1. You get consistent B2B product and configuration recommendations that align to your customers’ functional and business needs.
  2. You can drastically cut down on time, effort, and the need for technical know-how to generate a quote or shopping cart.
  3. You offer a genuine self-service e-commerce experience to your customers, reducing the need for sales rep assistance.
  4. You witness faster revenue generation by getting your new hires sale-ready sooner, matching the efficiency and effectiveness of veteran sales reps, and accelerating your expansion into new markets.

SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation

New enhancements to SAP Recommerce for your circular commerce business and sustainability strategy

In the Q1 2024 webcast, we took a deep-dive into SAP Recommerce. Since then, we’ve enhanced the overall ease of use and data visibility for both admin and workbench/business users.

For Admins:

  • You now have richer control over configurable product attributes. You can upload up to 15 customer-specific product attributes into SAP Recommerce alongside the default product data to cater to unique customer needs.
  • Here’s to greater visibility and transparency -- Admins and customers alike can now easily access sustainability data. Admins can import and view sustainability data for each SKU and search for specific products or metrics. Customers can view the imported sustainability data for each item they propose for trade-in via the shopping application.

For Workbench/Business Users:

  • Got a new item for check-in? Users can now manually enter or scan a new barcode to tag that item appropriately. Each item is given a distinct barcode or a digital ID (think: digital passport or serialization) for precise identification.
  • Dig into the details with this new feature—users can view a full list of all traded-in items and can search for specific items using the item’s barcode or unique ID value.
  • Users can effectively capture the default and actual time and cost a particular condition indicator takes to rectify, be it cleaning, repairing or reshaping.
  • Curate your custom workflows -- SAP Recommerce will draft out the steps needed for processing products from "Check In" to "Ready to Restock" based on condition severity, repair requirements, and possible programs (e.g., cleaning only, repair and clean, repair only). Users can select condition indicators that best reflect the evaluation of the product and choose the relevant process flow for the item—say, clean & repair. The subsequent steps get generated automatically and users can process the item until restock.

The overall benefits with these new enhancements are:

  1. Increased product usability across different lines of business and industry solutions
  2. Improved usability and management of trade-in products through intuitive business tools, search, product attribute data display, and flexible process flows
  3. Clear visibility of sustainability data and impact for both business users and customers

Create custom workflows in SAP Recommerce

Telco Accelerator - Empower customers to gain a more thorough understanding of their subscribed service with both fixed and personalized selections

We’ve improved the Telco Accelerator to help you transform faster with this industry-specific capability. Customers will have a more comprehensive understanding of their subscription services, for both fixed and personalized selections. Customers can also easily track modifications to their service contract selections, including pricing updates, providing greater transparency.

Optimize order fulfillment by managing order exceptions and viewing live On Time Performance metrics

Order Management Services now has a workflow to automate the handling of exceptions. If an issue happens during the fulfillment request, the system can re-trigger the fulfillment in some problematic status and apply actions such as re-sourcing or sending a notification to the employee about all these fulfillment requests.

Order Management Services can also now provide live insights for On Time Performance by aggregating all the order data in a single place.  Consume the insights through SAP Analytics Cloud or connect your favorite analytics tool to gain the desired visibility on your order lifecycle data.

Live insights for On Time Performance

Another enhancement is with SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability, which now supports using contextual updates to provide alternate sourcing results. This includes an updated customer promise for orders in process that cannot be fulfilled as initially sourced. Order sourcing can also respond at any time with multiple service codes for customers to choose the desired fulfillment methods such as standard or express deliveries.

The benefits with these updates are:

  1. Improving the ordering experience for customers, to react swiftly to exceptions occurring during order fulfillment
  2. Providing clear, immediate transparency of order KPIs and trends across all sales channels and fulfillment systems
  3. Understanding omnichannel sales trends early in time to react in other areas, such as promotions, merchandising, and marketing

 

Insightful

Intelligent e-commerce powered by AI

In Q4 2023, we introduced SAP CX AI Toolkit to help you infuse Generative AI for your commerce operations. SAP CX AI Toolkit now has a nifty new feature called "Ask the product". Now, shoppers can ask product-specific questions directly on the storefront! Yes, you read that right. Shoppers can receive answers generated by our very own Gen AI, using product data managed in the backend.

Think of the possibilities—whether it's seeking more information about product attributes and details, or unearthing manuals for intricate B2B products, the world's your oyster. The benefits of “Ask the product” are being able to:

  1. Efficiently locate solutions that are difficult to find within product documentation
  2. Transform your product attributes and descriptions into solutions for customers
  3. Improve your commerce experience with direct and immediate support to your storefront

We’ve also expanded Visual Search beyond the retail industry and into all industries. Visual Search improves product discovery by delivering quick and accurate search results based on AI image detection. So, let the searching spree begin!

 

 

“Ask the product” feature in SAP CX AI Toolkit

Personalize shopping experiences with a virtual AI advisor

New additions to the SAP Store enhance digital experiences by adding more innovation and services to our ever-expanding ecosystem. We are excited to spotlight the FrontNow partnership, giving your shoppers personalized experiences with the use of an AI advisor in customer interactions. This solution helps maximize conversion rates, increase average order value, and boost customer engagement.

FrontNow is a virtual AI advisor, who will joyfully represent your brand in engaging, one-on-one conversations. Shoppers can chat with this friendly advisor in a whopping 85 languages, obtaining tailor-made shopping and service experiences.

The magic behind this? FrontNow's unique ability to gauge customer interactions and delve deep into conversation intentions to self-discover fresh content areas and trend topics.

FrontNow Advisor multilanguage experience

 

Adaptive

Empowering customers with self-service capabilities to scale their infrastructure in non-production environments

At times, you may need to increase the performance of your testing environments. Enter SAP’s game-changing scalable sandboxes.

Our scalable sandboxes allow you to selectively ramp up or dial down specific services within non-production environments by allowing the modification of factors such as the number of instances, CPU, and memory requirements.

Furthermore, we're excited to announce SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits, which is a virtual currency. This virtual currency is sold as Capacity Units.  Using these capacity units, you can self-scale your non-production environments.  The SAP Commerce Cloud's consumption credits will be metered and you have complete usage visibility on SAP4Me (planned towards the end of 2024).

You can purchase the SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits through your Account Executive to cover the costs for any non-production related infrastructure scaling. Please note that Capacity Units depend on yearly consumption and any unused units at the close of the year won't be carried over.

With scalable sandboxes and SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits, customers are:

  1. Empowered with self-service capabilities to define your own infrastructure needs in non-productive environments
  2. Lowering costs by scaling only the infrastructure elements required (database, or VMs, etc.) and by scaling tenants down during quiet periods
  3. Simplifying purchase and consumption of these services through a simple and flexible commercial framework

If you're eager to dive into all the details about scalable sandboxes and Consumption Credits, you can hop over and tune into our recent webcast titled, "Your Key to Agile Scaling! SAP Commerce Cloud, Consumption Credits" webcast.

Capacity Units estimator

 

Safeguard your deployments when releasing significant updates

Currently, when deploying updates, our safeguards include testing a new release on development, QA, and staging environments before it takes center stage in production.

With blue-green deployments, customers can deploy a green environment right alongside a blue one to test it out before opening it to live traffic.

The game-changer here is the Canary deployment. This feature allows customers to transfer a desired percentage of live traffic to the green environment. Think of it like a soft launch to gain more faith in the new release's compatibility with live users. And guess what - this can be specified per endpoint and/or per service for added peace of mind. The benefits of this approach are:

  1. Reducing risk by controlling live customer traffic to new SAP Commerce Cloud deployments
  2. Gaining confidence in significant updates with the ability to smoothly roll-back a deployment with no downtime
  3. Validating your build and deployment quickly and easily

Canary deployment is only available if you subscribed to the previously mentioned SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits.

Ensure production environments remain online with Azure SQL Zonal Redundancy

To ensure that any disruption to Azure availability zones does not put production systems at risk, databases are now automatically replicated to different zones within the same region. The redundancy measure helps increase reliability for all customers.

This redundancy protection is our way of boosting reliability for all our customers, making sure your systems are always-on.

Level-up website security with two-factor authentication

Boost your website security with our enhanced two-factor authentication feature! You can now offer your customers an optional email verification for that extra layer of security during the sign-in process, making it significantly harder for attackers to break into accounts, even if credentials somehow fall into the wrong hands.

The best part is, you're always in the driver's seat – you can turn this verification process on or off whenever you want, by simply toggling a switch. The feature also allows you to set the time duration within which a user must input their one-time password, and the number of attempts they get.

We've also strengthened our password storage encryption. This leverages the latest password encoding strategies to further minimize the potential ramifications of any unforeseen data breaches. Even better, we give you the flexibility to customize your own secure strategy by allowing you to choose your preferred hashing mechanism. So, let's level-up your site security today!

Upgraded rules engine for more secure and stronger Commerce Promotions

We have now upgraded the rules engine for our Commerce Promotions with Drools 8. The upgrade enhances the level of security, improves GDPR compliance, and provides extended functionality. It also has backwards compatibility and delivers a more intuitive user experience.

There is an improved Decision Model and Notation (DMN) to ensure better compliance with the latest standards of DMN for robust decision modelling and execution. This update comes with a modern syntax for rules, which is designed to be more developer-friendly. Even without specialist knowledge, developers will find rule writing more intuitive in the integrated development environments (IDE).

We're also excited about Drools’ incorporated AI Integration capability, enabling customers to create advanced decision-making when plugging in machine learning engines. The new capability empowers businesses to carry out intelligent, adaptive, and autonomous business process automation.

Optimizing your Commerce solution with functional and technical assistance service

When it comes to guidance, our SAP experts are here to lend you or your partner a hand with our functional and technical assistance service. You can receive help from SAP CX experts with one-time, functional or technical expertise in a specified area, such as advisory, configuration, or feature activation. This service provides up to 16 hours of consulting support to help you get over unforeseen hurdles quickly.

Save time and gain control on restarting individual pods

You asked, and we listened. In this highly requested feature, administrators can now restart specific pods as required from the Portal without needing help of SAP Support teams. This saves time and gives customers greater control. You can also see a list of restarted pods to view the logs or do some troubleshooting. To avoid any potential disruption to service performance or availability, we recommend waiting for one pod to finish restarting before trying to restart another.

 

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources:

  • Q2 2024 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Readiness Kit
  • Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • SAP Commerce Cloud update cycle
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content

SAP Help Portal

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New

Composable Commerce

  • SAP Report: Achieving Agility in Digital Commerce: Guidance to Help Organizational Leaders Future-Proof Digital Experiences with an Agile Commerce Approach

 

Please share your feedback in a comment and follow our SAP Commerce Cloud topic page to connect with the community!

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Management
entry.content:
This blog provides a broad outline on how to integrate an organization`s greenhouse gas (GHG) emission data from SAP EHS Management, Environment Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT). 

What is SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT):

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) provides organization with the platform and capabilities to enable organization to monitor its sustainability performance overtime and to see if organizations are meeting sustainability ambitions. 

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) - Organization`s Insights

 

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) offers various ways of importing data and one of the ways is using SAP EHS Management, Environment Management to import greenhouse gas (GHG) emission data for a given period of time.

Fact Sheet:

 

Let`s just walk through the business use case:

I have got to steer sustainability performance using SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT). As part of ESG reporting, one of the crucial aspects is to report out GHG emissions with respect to organization`s business activities. So, as an Environmental Manager, I have got to calculate Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 GHG Emissions using SAP EHS Environmental Management and import the calculated GHG Emissions data into SAP SCT.

My organization is a process industry, we process Anthracite Coal, which has high percentage of fixed carbon and a low percentage of volatile matter and use Natural Gas for heating purpose. We also purchase electricity from the grid and our employees commute by train and car on daily basis. My organization do have own fleet and uses gasoline for the run.

Using SAP EHS Environment Management, I have calculated Scope 1, Scope 2 and Scope 3 (Category 7 - Employee Commuting) and using "Import Data From SAP EHS Management" application, the GHG emissions have been imported into SAP SCT.

Disclaimer:

This blog does not cover the creation aspects of SAP EHS Management, Environment Management - Master data, Emissions Data Collection,  Calculation of CO2e  and  Location Aggregation. This covers the integration of greenhouse gas (GHG) emission data from SAP EHS Management, Environment Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT).

I have posted a few blogs on SAP EHS Management, Environment Management, which provide a high-level overview on creation aspects of Master Data, Data Collection, Calculation of CO2e and Location Aggregation, and are available on SCN and can be accessed using below links.

  • SAP S/4HANA EHS Environment Management, Emission Management - Master Data Entities 
  • SAP S/4HANA EHS Environment Management, Emission Management - Identifying & Preparing Compliance Requirement 
  • SAP S/4HANA EHS Environment Management, Emission Management - Compliance Scenario Activities 

Let us dive in...

With the Integration of SAP Sustainability Control Tower with SAP EHS Management, an organization can

  • Import recorded GHG emissions from the SAP EHS Management system
  • Associate activity data with GHG scopes and sub-categories based on pre-defined data classifiers
  • Assign GHG emissions data to respective business locations in SAP Sustainability Control Tower
  • Analyze GHG Emissions data in the "View Emission Dashboard" app.
  • Set and Track emission goals in the "Ambitions" app.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower - Data Management and Integration

 

Prerequisites:

  • SAP EHS Management, Environment Management is active in the SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.
  • SAP SCT must have connected to SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/HANA Cloud system.
    • On premise SAP S/4HANA: Set up the cloud connector, which allows secure communication between the on-premise system and the SAP BTP subaccount. Once the SAP Cloud Connector is setup, configure the HTTP destination service on SAP BTP.
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Configure the HTTP destination service on SAP BTP to enable data replication between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and the SAP BTP Subaccount.
    • Required Versions: SAP S/4HANA 2022 or Later ; SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2022 or Later (Private); SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public  Edition always requires the latest available version. 
  • Role SUS_SCT_EHS_Data_Manager must be assigned

Which Data is Imported:

Organization can import GHG Emissions and offsets from calculation results, data collections, and location aggregations when the following restrictions are included.

  • Only data for the listed substance "Carbon Dioxide Equivalent" in metric tons is imported

  • Only Approved, Non-Preliminary data is imported

  • Data must be assigned to locations of type "Plant" or "Site". The locations must have a plant ID.

  • Ensure that the location is maintained as a "Business Location" with hierarchy in SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

 

  • Emissions data with GHG Data Classifiers:

Data Classifiers in SAP EHS Environment Management act as a type of tag which mark the type of data and what the emission is being used. This tagging is used by SAP Sustainability Control Tower to populate the properties of the emission DPI (Data Provider Interface) table.

I have assigned respective GHG Data Classifiers to Scope 1, Scope 2 & Scope 3 emissions during compliance scenario execution in SAP EHS Environment Management as per SAP Sustainability Control Tower emissions source category.

GHG data collection & Calculations in SAP EHS Environment Management,

Now, let us have a glance at assignment of GHG Data Classifiers and Calculated Data for above shown Compliance Scenario Calculations,

Scope 1 Emissions: Process Emissions

Scope 1 Emissions: Mobile Combustion

Scope 2 Emissions: Location Based (Purchased Electricity)

Scope 2 Emissions: Market Based (Purchased Electricity)

Scope 3 Emissions: Employee Commuting

How is the Data Imported:

Once the GHG Emissions inventory is calculated against respective compliance scenario, the calculated data is imported using CDS views. Organization can analyze the GHG Emissions data using out of the box functionality before importing the GHG Emissions into SAP SCT.

View Browser app. in SAP EHS Environment Management provides a list of all available CDS views and can be used to analyze calculated GHG Emissions data that will be imported into SAP SCT.

CDS Views:

let us have a glance at the calculated GHG Emissions data using View Browser app.

GHG Emissions Data Analysis:

Graphical Display of GHG Emissions Data:

Heat Map

 

Import GHG Data From SAP EHS Management into SAP SCT:

 

Select a period for importing data from SAP EHS Management,

Once the import has been finished , validate the Imported Data - we will be navigated to CPE (Calculation and Processing Engine) environment to validate and publish the data. 

Process Template

 

View Emissions Dashboard in SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT):

 

 

 

let us have a glance at "Gross GHG Emissions - Scope 1" ambition for the business location "Louisiana".

Current performance for all the Business Locations

let us drill down to check whether Louisiana is off-track or on-track,

Louisiana location is on-track with respect to Scope-1 Emissions

Graphical Representation of Scope 1 Emissions for location "Louisiana"

 

This brings me to the end of topic on integrating greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions data from SAP EHS Management, Environment Management into SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT). 😊 I hope this blog proves useful to SAP SCT & EHS users, and look forward to seeing organizations using this integration to report out their GHG emissions.

"Nature is not a place to visit, it Is Home - Gary Snyder"

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: Hi,Any news on increase of 401K Catch-Up Contribution Limit for Participants 60-63 ?
entry.content:
Is SAP doing some code/config change for this change below ?

Increase Age 50 401(k) Catch-Up Contribution Limit for Participants Ages 60 through 63
Under current law, employees who attain age 50 or older are permitted to make catch-up contributions in the Retirement Savings Plan in excess of the otherwise applicable salary deferral limits. The limit on catch-up contributions for 2023 is $7,500.
Effective January 1, 2025, the catch-up limit for employees who have attained ages 60, 61, 62, and 63 will be the greater of (1) $10,000 (indexed) or (2) 150% of the regular catch-up contribution limit

entry.title: Downtime on Saturday, August 31, and Sunday, September 1, Due to Migration of SAP SQL Anywhere Forum
entry.content:
On Saturday, August 31, we will begin migrating the questions and answers from the SAP SQL Anywhere forum to SAP Community.  During this migration, public content on SAP Community will remain read-only, but log-in to the platform will be unavailable. Therefore, you will be unable to post content, leave kudos, or do anything else requiring log-in, including accessing gated content and closed groups (e.g., partner and SAP SuccessFactors groups). 

We expect to complete the migration by the end of Sunday, September 1, at which point you will be able to access SAP Community normally again. 

In preparation for the migration, the SAP SQL Anywhere forum will be shut down on Thursday, August 29. After the migration, the forum's questions and answers will appear in SAP Community with the SAP SQL Anywhere tag. The old forum links will redirect to the appropriate SAP Community posts and feeds. 

If you're a member of SAP Community and the SAP SQL Anywhere forum, your forum questions and answers will be added to your community content automatically at migration -- just as long as your SAP Universal ID includes the email address associated with your user profile for the SAP SQL Anywhere forum. (You can learn more about SAP Universal ID on this site, and you can get support on this site.) 

We'll add an announcement banner to SAP Community before the migration begins, to remind you of the migration and its impact on the site.

entry.title: HCM Brasil: eSocial – Nota técnica S-1.2 Nº 04/2024
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Na última segunda-feira, dia 22 de julho, entregamos a solução da Nota Técnica S-1.2 04/2024, promovendo importantes modificações no leiaute do eSocial para inclusão do eConsignado.

Nessa atualização, foram alterados os seguintes eventos:

  • S-1010 - Tabela de rubricas 
  • S-1200 - Remuneração de trabalhador vinculado ao Regime Geral de Previd. Social 
  • S-2299 - Desligamento 
  • S-2399 - Trabalhador sem vínculo de emprego - término 
  • S-5003 - Informações do FGTS por trabalhador 

Para que seus sistemas estejam alinhados com as modificações implementadas pelo governo, é necessário instalar as seguintes SAP Notes:

  • 3491190 - eSocial - S-1010 - Technical Note S-1.2 Nº 04/2024 - eConsignado adjustments
  • 3491336 - eSocial NT 04/2024 - S-5003 – eConsignado
  • 3495073 - eSocial - S-1200/S-2299/S-2399 - Technical Note S-1.2 Nº 04/2024 - eConsignado adjustments

Após a instalação dessas notas, sua empresa estará apta a enviar as informações relacionadas ao eConsignado. Vale ressaltar que, até o momento, não há informações oficiais sobre a operação do eConsignado por parte das empresas e sobre como essa nova modalidade de empréstimos será conduzida. Estamos atentos a quaisquer novas atualizações e compartilharemos as mesmas com vocês assim que estiverem disponíveis.

Lembramos que as alterações legais que impactam a localização de folha de pagamento para Brasil sempre são disponibilizadas no Regulatory Change Manager, a nova ferramenta da SAP para que você possa acompanhar o planejamento das alterações legais que impactam os produtos.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,

Anderson Marques

entry.title: Re: Task 9 - Using CQL in an unbound action (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content:
After some debugging I found out what happened. Here is a snipped from my main.js

const db = await cds.connect.to('db');
const { Products } = db.entities; 

const productCountQuery = await SELECT.one.from(Products).columns('count(*)');
console.log(productCountQuery);

const productCountQuery2 = await SELECT.one.from(Products).columns('count(*) as count');
console.log(productCountQuery2);

console.log-Entries local in BAS:

{ count: 77 }
{ count: 77 }

console.log Entries from CF:

{ 'count ( * )': 77 }
{ count: 77 }

So why do I get a different result here?

By the way: the Error "‌‌️️❗️Uncaught Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'ProductName')" was because I used the undefined "count" for the modulo calculation, leaving me selecting the product with ProductID = "NaN"

const productCount = productCountQuery.count;
const charCodesCount = this.getCharCodesCount(communityid);
const productId = charCodesCount % productCount + 1;

const productQuery = await SELECT.one.from(Products, productId, product => { product.ProductName });
return productQuery.ProductName;

entry.title: Re: Brazil Localization - Send of Nfse PDF to the customer
entry.content:
Hello @EduardoPaim.

As explained in the SAP Help Portal page Electronic Service Nota Fiscal (NFS-e):

"Non-Supported Features

The following features aren’t supported:

  • Generating a TXT or XML file for manual contingency processing.
  • Sending the NFS-e via e-mail, as this is done by the tax authority."

Therefore, this is currently not supported by SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil solution. 

If you need to request this feature request, please submit an Enhancement Request / Feature Request in the SAP Customer Influence portal SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, invoicing option for Brazil (nota fiscal eletrônica).

Regards.

entry.title: 10 new items added to SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for 2408 release
entry.content:
In this blog post, we want to share the 10 highlights of the methodology content enhancements in SAP Activate for deployment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that were recently published for the 2408 release update. Note that you can find an approximately 5-minute video showing a demo of all these enhancements at the bottom of this post.

1. Initial Admin User/Technical Administrator

You will find a new task that covers the verification of the Initial Admin/Technical Administrator, including defining their role, outlining steps to identify the suitable candidate, introducing a new user onboarding token process, guiding on creating and using SAP Universal IDs and managing cloud notification subscriptions on behalf of others. You can access the here - Verify Initial Admin/Technical Administrator. See more details about the task in the image below.

2. New Bundled Provisioning Process

A new task has been created to streamline the process of requesting initial system access for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and related systems, including SAP Cloud ALM tenant, SAP Central Business Configuration tenant, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Starter System. Requests are managed through SAP for Me, initiated by the initial Admin User, who subsequently receives access instructions via email. Bundled Provisioning allows partners and customers to request access to multiple system tenants in a single request for enhanced efficiency. You can access the task here - Request Initial System Access of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

3. Grant Access to Partner for Creating and Managing Cases

A new SAP Activate task explains granting SAP implementation partners access to create and manage cases for customers on their behalf. The procedure, detailed in SAP Note 2518600, outlines steps to follow using SAP for Me, enabling authorization for service partners (S-users) to provide support services. You can access the task here Grant Access to Partner for Creating and Managing Cases.

4. Data Migration Updates

The recent updates emphasize the importance of downtime and restriction windows in data migration, ensuring minimal business disruption. A new accelerator, Software Lifecycle Management, was added to support data migration tasks in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition development environment. Additionally, the Hotfix Collection Deployment Restrictions.pdf accelerator was introduced for the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit task, offering guidance on deploying hotfixes. Additional content updates were made to enhance support for data migration via direct transfer, and general procedure updates improved readability and usability, aiming to streamline the migration process and enhance user comprehension. You can find the updated content in the following tasks:

  • Execute Data Migration in the Development Tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Self-Enable on SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit
  • Execute Data Migration in the Development Tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud

5. Organizational Change Management (OCM) with SAP Activate

A comprehensive review of the Organizational Change Management (OCM) content included new and rework of many deliverables, tasks and accelerators. In addition, a new Organizational Change Management tag has been created that includes 7 accelerators in Discover phase attached to task Organizational Change Management Overview; 12 accelerators in Prepare phase assigned to following tasks:

  • Change Concept
  • Change Plan
  • Stakeholder Analysis
  • Communication Analysis and Plan

an accelerator in Explore phase assigned to task Communication Plan Execution; 4 accelerators in the Realize phase assigned to the task Detailed Change Impact Analysis. 
Additionally, you can find a standalone comprehensive methodology roadmap for Organizational Change Management in SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer.

6. Lifecycle Events and Hotfix Collection

The SAP Activate methodology updates for lifecycle events include adding tasks to the Release and Update Cycle deliverable, covering three key lifecycle events: Review and Process Hotfix Collection, Evaluate and Activate Feature from Continuous Delivery, and Prepare and Process Upgrade. These tasks streamline lifecycle management, and the SAP Activate for Upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) roadmap is now linked to the Prepare and Process Upgrade task, offering a structured guide for handling upgrades in a 3-system landscape.

The Review and Process Hotfix Collection task was updated to include references to several key accelerators: Software Lifecycle Management for Customers, Upgrade & Maintenance Schedule, and Hotfix Collection Deployment Restrictions. These updates provide users with comprehensive guidance on managing hotfix collections effectively. You can access them on the following tasks:

  • Software Lifecycle Management for Customers
  • Upgrade & Maintenance Schedule
  • Hotfix Collection Deployment Restrictions

 

7. Parallel Project Line

The enhancements for customers in the early adopter program for parallel project lines allow for a clear separation of incremental configuration activities from the “main line,” enabling the productive environment to remain up-to-date with the latest software releases. After validation, new configurations can be merged back into the main line and transported to the productive environment. These enhancements also include features for managing development and test environments, copying master data for data migration testing, and copying automated testing scripts. You can access this content in a dedicated SAP Activate methodology for parallel project lines early adopters here. If you want to see only the tasks that have been adjusted for this new functionality, access Parallel Project Line tag to display these tasks.

8. SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Business Technology Platform

The SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides implementation guidelines for customers deploying BTP-based solutions. It includes phases, deliverables, and tasks following the SAP Activate framework, with accelerators linked to relevant tasks for easy access. Deliverables and tasks are listed sequentially, though some may need to be parallelized for efficiency. This roadmap will be rolled out in phases, starting with a focus on governance and key implementation milestones, and later expanding to specialized areas like App Development, Automation, Integration, Data & Analytics, and AI.

9. SAP Activate for Conversion of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system to 3-system landscape

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition roadmap for converting from a 2-system to a 3-system landscape has been updated with insights from customer conversions using both CBC and Solution Builder-based systems. Key updates include setting up transport management for business continuity, revised self-enablement activities, and the addition of a new accelerator highlighting delta features for the landscape conversion. A Customer Conversion Checklist accelerator and a new SAP Community accelerator for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape, have also been introduced to support the conversion process.

10. Find Roadmap Viewer from Google and Bing Searches and access it from SAP for Me

Watch the video below to see how to access SAP Activate methodology content from your SAP for Me screen. It adds to the searching for the SAP Activate content on traditional search engines like Google Search or Microsoft Bing.

You can view the demo of there 10 enhancements in the video following this link - https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_djrp3uui

 

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Did you miss the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud – Allocations Management Migration Update
entry.content:
With QRC1 2024, we restricted the usage of the legacy Allocation Designer for designing new allocation processes and offered a manual migration path to data actions (more details can be found here). Continuing our product journey of gradually discontinuing the legacy Allocation Designer and running structured allocation processes as an integral part of data actions, we reached the next milestone of automatically migrating all legacy allocations to data actions.  

QRC3 - Important Updates Coming

With the SAP Analytics Cloud upgrade to QRC3, the legacy allocation processes and steps will be automatically copied and migrated to data actions, facilitating an easy transition from the legacy allocation functionality. Using the data actions going forward for defining and executing the structured allocation processes brings additional benefits, such as parameterizable data action triggers, multi-action execution, and automated planning processes (visit SAP help for more details).

The migration will ensure a smooth transition by maintaining the following:

  • The data action representations of the legacy allocations will retain all existing relations between allocation steps, processes and data actions embedded legacy allocations.
  • Each allocation process is copied and migrated to a dedicated data action. Its properties, like name and description, are transferred to this data action for easy retrieval. The process's legacy allocation steps are migrated as data action steps.
  • The legacy allocation steps used multiple times in different allocation processes and/or data actions will be created as nested data actions.
  • The privileges transfer is part of the migration. This means the corresponding created data actions will be governed by the same user- and team- privileges as the original legacy allocations. A Planning Professional license is required to access, edit, and execute data actions. 

The automatically created data actions will be accessible from the newly created Files > My Files > Public > Structured Allocations folder.

Image 1 Migrated legacy allocations

 Note: You will retain view-only access to legacy allocations through the Allocation Legacy Designer. However, editing legacy allocation processes or steps will no longer be possible.

Image 2 Using the Legacy Designer does not allow creating new allocations

Although the execution of legacy allocations is still supported, we strongly advise updating your legacy structured allocation execution workflows. As a reminder, users triggering legacy allocations from stories will receive a notification advising them to transition to data actions.

Image 3 Message displayed upon legacy allocation execution

The final phase of discontinuing legacy allocations is expected with the QRC4 update. Then, we will no longer support the execution of legacy allocations. Please use the automatically migrated data action representations or manually migrate your allocations in the oncoming release period (QRC3 until QRC4) and update your planning workflows accordingly. We plan to retire the legacy designer completely with QRC1 in 2025.

Please use the Commenting section if you have any questions or require assistance with the structure allocations transition to data actions!

entry.title: Buenos Aires - SAP Stammtisch 15.8.2024 18:00 ART #SAPCodeJamsLATAM
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Buenos Aires has a chance to meet after our first of two local SAP CodeJams!

SAP Stammtisch in Buenos Aires will happen on August 15th, 2024 at 18:00 local time for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", we don't have a reservation at the location - Keller Bier but we can meet there. FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the SAP Developer Advocates Latin America Roadshow

entry.title: How to view PO Adobe form in XML format

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to view PO Adobe form in XML format
entry.content:
Hello!

You can view/extract the XML format of an output form for PO using app Change Purchase Orders - Advance, via the 'Messages' output screen.

Add a new output item and edit the Print Queue to #&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&# and then use display document button to show the XML data.

To highlight best practices around this #&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&# method to extract XML data, you may refer to below pointers:

  • This works only for applications that have included the standard output control reuse screen in edit mode (for example, Manage Billing Documents, Change Sales Orders -VA02, Change Billing Documents, Change Purchase Orders - Advance, etc).
  • This works only for output items in status 'In Preparation'.
  • The XML always returns the current data of the underlying business document.
  • Does not work for duplicates.
  • The XML does not include data from the master form template.
  • An output with print queue #&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&# is not possible.
  • Print queue #&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&#Â cannot be used in output parameter determination.

KBA 0002825919 also explains the same.

entry.title: CAP Build with Workspaces and package-lock.json
entry.content:
Hi,

We are starting to use the beta custom build feature of npm Workspaces. Is there an intended approach of dealing with freezing of imported packages?

Without the npm workspaces, this is handled by "cds build" (it copies the package-lock.json file of the CAP project into the gen/srv folder which in turn ensures the build reproduces with the previously used package versions).

With the "cds build --ws-pack" option however, we find that:

  • The package-lock.json file lives in the root of the project structure (i.e. not in the individual workspace folders)
  • When "cds build --ws-pack" is run from within a workspace folder, there is no package-lock.json file copied into the gen/srv folder
  • The resulting deployment package does not resolve the same package versions between builds (it upgrades within the allowed semantic versioning specifications)

We work around this currently using a custom build script like this. It works except if the included workspaces have competing version requirements (i.e. to the root package-lock.json file has something like "subfolder/node_modules/somepackage") - then a mere copy of the package-lock.json file does not solve it.

#!/bin/bash -e
npx cds build --ws-pack --production
# Include the top-level package-lock file
cp ../package-lock.json gen/srv

Is there any intended approach or advice on this? The npm workspace feature will be very useful, thanks for the work going into it!

Regards

//Carl

entry.title: Timeout of 120000ms exceeded, Unable to fulfill proxy request
entry.content:
I'm putting together a CSV import using Build apps, with a Desitination pointing to a python app deployed on BTP.

Everything works fine, until I hit the 120000ms (2 minute) timeout when processing a large file, at which point the Build app throws an error.

Checking the logs, the python app is still working through the file after the 2 minute timeout, and completes fine, it looks to be the BTP DESTINATION itself which is timing out and reporting an error when there is none.

I'm not sure if the issue is down to DESTINATION parameters, or with SAP BUILD apps itself.

What timeout parameters are available to set/try?

I've tried HTML5.Timeout, Timeout, and ClientReadTimeout. Nothing seems to extend anything over 2 mins.

 POST https://ndbs-build.preview.eu10.apps.build.cloud.sap/destinations/SIMILARITY_SEARCH_LOADER_NO_AUTH 500 (Internal Server Error)

entry.title: Re: SAC Excel MEMERPROPERTY not returning Description
entry.content:
Thank you for your time and help. As FYI, I also submitted a ticket specifically for pulling in a dimension member 'Description' because this is not a custom property. Response received is below. The first option below is what stopped working with Office 365 change (upper case 'D'). I changed to lower case 'd' and works for Users. 
Regards r.a.

--------------------------------
I checked this with Development team and the case is now sorted out.
The KBA 3497065 was updated accordingly.
In summary, we have four scenarios for using Description in the formula.

Below examples work because property description is used and it is not case sensitive:
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY( DS_1, "Account" , 0123, "Description")
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY( DS_1, "Account" , 0123, "description")

Below example works because property ID is used exactly as it is:
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY( DS_1, "Account" , 0123, "[Account].description")

Below example does NOT work because property ID is case sensitive:
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY( DS_1, "Account" , 0123, "[Account].Description")

Please check if the above also applies to your case. As per the KBA, we strongly recommend using property ID to retrieve the Description property to allow multiple languages.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Detailed Phased approach for BW/4HANA In-place conver...
entry.content: Hi All, Thanks for your replies, The reason I mention process chains is that we used the "Start BW/4 Transfer Tool" icon in each process chain to create our conversion Task List in STC01. We have multiple process chains for Master data Atrribute by Functonal area, then multiple MD Text loads by Functonal area and finally multiple Transaction Data. Therefore, when we complete each SAP_BW4_TRANSFER_INPLACE conversion, we are completing each process chain. Therefore, when we complete a Scenario in Development and QA, which would be a number of process chains, and then Go-live in Production, would we have a number of Process Chains from the 1st Scenario running with ODP and the other process chains still running with SAPI? Additionally, while we are completing each Scenario, do we remain in Compatibility mode throughout, because you need to complete all the scenarios before we have completed Step "3 - During Transfer Tool" in the B4HANA Readiness Check (RSB4HCONV) tool?

entry.title: Re: YCOA standard chart of accounts, environment: SAP S4 HANA CLOUD - Partner Demo Customizing?
entry.content: You can create a new account by means of copying it from an existing GL account. There is no way to create a GL account from scratch. Also keep in mind that this master records are transportable, you cannot update the chart of accounts directly in production.
